ddls:
CREATE TABLE "log_files" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the log file */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log file (e.g., Log, Windows Initialization) */,
  "software_program_id" INTEGER /* ID of the software program that generated the log file */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the software program */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the log file in bytes */,
  "file_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location of the log file */,
  "log_format_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log file format */,
  "log_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log type */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the log file was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the log file was last modified */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to ensure data integrity */,
  "compression_status" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the log file is compressed */,
  "compression_format" TEXT /* Format of the compression (e.g., gzip, zip) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_files_file_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_location_id") REFERENCES log_locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_files_log_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_format_id") REFERENCES log_file_formats ("format_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_files_software_program_id FOREIGN KEY ("software_program_id") REFERENCES software_programs ("program_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_files_log_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_type_id") REFERENCES log_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "software_programs" (
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each software program */,
  "program_name" TEXT /* Name of the software program */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the software program */,
  "developer" TEXT /* Name of the developer or company that created the software program */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the software program was released */,
  "support_email" TEXT /* Email address for support queries related to the software program */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the log type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the log type (1 for low, 5 for high) */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates whether this log type is a default type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log location */,
  "location_path" TEXT /* Path to the log file */,
  "is_remote" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the log location is remote */,
  "remote_server" TEXT /* Name or IP address of the remote server */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method used to access the log file (e.g., FTP, SFTP, HTTP) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, administrator, support) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log file being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the log file */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the log file was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the log file */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES log_files ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_file_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log file version */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log file */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the log file */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the log file was versioned */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of changes in this version */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created this version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Indicates whether this version is the current version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_file_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_file_versions_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES log_files ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON or comma-separated list of permissions associated with the role */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the role has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_file_formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log file format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the log file format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log file format */,
  "is_binary" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the log file format is binary */,
  "default_encoding" TEXT /* Default encoding for the log file format (e.g., UTF-8, ASCII) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)
